title: Fall 2026 internship applications are open — come bake with us
excerpt: Our 12-week paid internship program returns this September. Past interns have gone on to Michelin-starred kitchens, food science PhDs, and their own bakeries. You'll rotate through all five stations, develop your own cookie flavor as a final project, and get mentorship from Lena and Priya. $22/hr, 30 hrs/week. Applications close August 15.

title: Summer 2026: Berry Cobbler returns, and we're testing a new stone fruit flavor
excerpt: Oregon berry season is here. Marcus has already been out foraging — the first flats of marionberries arrived this morning. The Berry Cobbler cookie is back on the menu for its limited 8-week run. Meanwhile, Priya and Olivia are in the test kitchen with peaches, pluots, and something Priya calls 'a stone fruit situation.' No details yet. The kitchen smells incredible.

title: Carlos Mendez says goodbye — 340 cookies a night, two years, one legacy
excerpt: Carlos ran his last night shift on Wednesday. He set a kitchen record of 340 cookies in a single shift, managed our overnight cold-proofing for two years, and never once let a tray leave the oven early. He's moving to Mexico City to open his own bakery — a panadería with a cookie program inspired by Big Cookies. We're heartbroken and proud in equal measure. Carlos, you're always family.

title: Factory Open Day: 60 visitors, one proposal, and a new single-day cookie record
excerpt: Our spring Open Day broke every record. 60 visitors packed the kitchen, Marcus ran four sold-out demos, and someone proposed to their partner in front of the cooling racks. (She said yes.) The team also baked 512 cookies in a single day for the first time — a new kitchen record. Thanks to everyone who came out. Next Open Day in October.

title: Two years of Big Cookies — a letter from Lena
excerpt: Two years ago today, we opened a 400-square-foot kitchen with one mixer, one oven, and a waitlist of 200. Today we're 9 bakers in a 3,000-square-foot bakery, shipping to 50 states and Canada. But some things haven't changed: the butter is still European, the chocolate is still single-origin, and every tray still gets a human QC before it leaves. 'The waiting list is gone,' Lena writes, 'but the obsession isn't.' Happy birthday to us. Thank you for two incredible years.

title: Valentine's 2026: bigger boxes, earlier pre-order, zero disappointed customers
excerpt: We learned from last year's 6-hour sellout. This Valentine's season we made 400 gift boxes — double the 2025 run — and opened pre-orders a full week early. Result: boxes were available for 48 hours instead of 6, and Sofia didn't have to make any apologetic phone calls. Progress.

title: Thomas Berg hits 50,000 cookies — and gets his name on the Hobart mixer
excerpt: Thomas runs the morning bake: 200 cookies before 8am, every day, since he joined in 2024. On Tuesday he hit a cumulative 50,000 cookies. The team commemorated the milestone by engraving his name on the Hobart mixer with a Dremel. 'Consistency is the only thing that matters,' Thomas said. 'Also butter. Consistency and butter.'

title: 2025 by the numbers: 32,000 cookies, 1 major award, and a lot of cold butter
excerpt: Year two is in the books. We shipped to all 50 states plus Canada, launched five new flavors (Masala Chai, English Toffee Crunch, Lemon Lavender, Maple Pecan, and the August debut of our Berry Cobbler seasonal), won Best Artisan Cookie at the National Bakery Awards, and grew to 9 team members. Carlos set a new single-shift record of 340 cookies. The mixer is getting its annual spa day on January 2. Happy New Year from all of us.

title: We're moving to a bigger kitchen in January
excerpt: After nearly two years in our original space, we've signed a lease on a 3,000-square-foot bakery three blocks away in southeast Portland. Double the oven capacity, a dedicated packing station for Diego, and an actual break room. 'We outgrew the 400-square-foot kitchen about 18 months ago,' Lena admits. The new space opens January 2026. Operations continue uninterrupted during the move.

title: Holiday ordering 2025: bigger production, same 48-hour wait
excerpt: Last year's holiday season was our first — and we learned a lot. This year we've doubled our butter order (again), James has reinforced the gift box with a new insert design, and we're running a second shipping day on Wednesdays. Order by December 17 for Christmas delivery. Newsletter subscribers get early access starting Friday.

title: Fall Open Day: James's packaging demo steals the show
excerpt: Our second Open Day of 2025 drew 45 visitors. Marcus demoed the Classic Chocolate Chunk from start to finish. But the surprise hit was James's packaging station — he showed the crush-proof box system, drop-tested a package from six feet (it survived), and let visitors try the hand-stamping. Diego's QC station also had a line. Next Open Day: April 2026.

title: Maple Pecan launches — Lena's Vermont weekend becomes a cookie
excerpt: Lena spent a weekend in Vermont last fall and came back with a gallon of maple syrup and an idea. The Maple Pecan cookie combines grade-A dark amber maple syrup, toasted pecans, and brown butter in a soft-baked base. 'It tastes like a Vermont farmhouse in October,' she says. Available as a fall seasonal through November.

title: Berry Cobbler: Oregon summer in a cookie, with foraged marionberries
excerpt: Our newest seasonal celebrates Oregon's legendary berry harvest. Marcus personally foraged the first batch of marionberries from a farm outside Hood River. The cookie combines a vanilla-brown-butter base with whole berries and a crumble topping inspired by a classic cobbler. 'This is the cookie version of an Oregon summer,' Priya says. Available through September while berries last.

title: Lemon Lavender is here — and it sold out in 4 hours
excerpt: After 47 test batches and months of anticipation, the Lemon Lavender summer seasonal launched this morning. The first production run — 180 cookies — sold out by 10am. Olivia, who developed the recipe, was not surprised. 'I've tasted this cookie 47 times and I still want another one,' she said. Second batch drops tomorrow at 9am. The 8-week run has officially begun.

title: New Summer Flavor: Lemon Lavender drops July 1
excerpt: After 47 test batches (Olivia's relentless R&D), Priya has finally signed off on our summer seasonal flavor of 2025. Meyer lemon zest meets culinary lavender in a brown-butter base. "It tastes like a summer garden smells," she says. Available for a limited 8-week run.

title: Masala Chai cookie launched — the kitchen smelled like Mumbai for a week
excerpt: Priya's rainy-day experiment is finally on the menu. She steeped chai spices directly into brown butter, then folded the infused butter into the dough. Cardamom, ginger, cinnamon, black pepper, and a hint of clove. Thomas said the kitchen smelled so good he almost didn't want to go home. Available year-round.

title: We won Best Artisan Cookie at the National Bakery Awards
excerpt: The Classic Chocolate Chunk took top honors in a blind tasting against 200+ entries. The judges' comment: "Unprecedented depth of flavor. The 48-hour cold fermentation produces a complexity we've never encountered in a chocolate chip cookie."

title: English Toffee Crunch joins the permanent menu after a year of testing
excerpt: Priya has been iterating on this one since May 2024 — 12 attempts to get the toffee crunch just right without shattering during shipping. Inspired by an English sweets shop, the cookie combines homemade almond toffee with a brown-butter dough. "It took 12 tries," Priya says, "but the 13th was perfect."

title: Factory Open Day: 40 visitors, 300 cookies, zero leftovers
excerpt: Our April Open Day was the busiest yet. Marcus ran three sold-out baking demos. Diego's packaging station was the surprise hit. Next one on the Open Day page.

title: Now shipping to Canada
excerpt: After months of navigating customs forms, we now ship to all Canadian provinces. Delivery takes 3-5 days. Cookies stay fresh for 5 days at room temp — same as our domestic orders.

title: Valentine's Day gift boxes sell out in 6 hours
excerpt: Our limited-edition Valentine's box — 6 Classic Chocolate Chunks, 6 Double Dark Chocolates, in a hand-stamped red kraft box with a handwritten note — sold all 200 units before noon. Sofia personally called everyone who missed out and offered a rain check with free shipping. "Next year we'll make more," she promises.

title: Meet Diego: our new Pack & Ship Lead
excerpt: Diego joins us from a ceramics studio, where he developed an almost unreasonable attention to detail. Every box now gets a hand-stamped logo and a personal QC check.

title: 2024 by the numbers: 18,000 cookies, 9 flavors, and one very tired mixer
excerpt: Our first (partial) year in business is in the books. We shipped to 42 states, launched 4 new flavors, grew the team from 2 to 12, and set a single-day record of 340 cookies (Carlos's night shift, November 14). The Hobart mixer is getting a well-deserved maintenance check on January 2. Thank you, Portland. See you in 2025.

title: Sea Salt Caramel voted 'Dessert of the Year' by City Food Magazine readers
excerpt: 12,000+ readers voted in City Food Magazine's annual poll, and our Sea Salt Caramel took the top dessert spot. The editors noted: "A 70-year-old family recipe from Brittany, made in a Portland kitchen by a Le Cordon Bleu-trained baker — it doesn't get more authentic than this." We also earned a Sustainability Star from the Green Kitchen Alliance for 100% compostable packaging.

title: Holiday ordering is open — and we've learned from last year's chaos
excerpt: Last year we didn't exist yet, so this is our first holiday season. We've tripled our butter order, added a second shipping day, and James has designed a festive gift box that survived his signature drop-test from six feet. Order by December 18 for Christmas delivery. Early access for newsletter subscribers starts today.

title: We won Small Business of the Year from the Regional Chamber of Commerce
excerpt: From 2 employees to 12 in under a year, while maintaining a 4.9-star average across 2,400+ reviews. The Chamber cited our commitment to fair-trade sourcing, living-wage employment, and the fact that we've never shipped a cookie we wouldn't eat ourselves. The plaque is hanging above the mixer.

title: Raspberry Dark: a happy accident becomes a permanent menu item
excerpt: Marcus was experimenting with freeze-dried fruit additions when he dropped a tray of raspberries into the dark chocolate dough. Rather than discard it, he baked it. The result — tart raspberry bursts against deep dark chocolate — was so good that Priya fast-tracked it to the permanent menu. Sometimes the best flavors come from a spill.

title: Matcha White Chocolate: a Tokyo transplant and an intern's final project
excerpt: Emma Larsson, our first pastry intern, developed this cookie as her capstone project — a collaboration with a ceremonial-grade matcha producer in Uji, Japan, arranged by Yuki. The result is an earthy-sweet cookie with a vivid green hue that's almost too pretty to eat. Emma graduated last week and has accepted a position at a Michelin-starred restaurant in Copenhagen.

title: Portland Monthly calls us 'the best cookie you'll wait 48 hours for'
excerpt: Our first major press feature dropped today. Portland Monthly sent a writer who spent a full day in the kitchen — from dough mixing at 5am to the final QC check at 4pm. The headline says it all. Web traffic tripled overnight. Sofia is answering emails at midnight.

title: Double Dark Chocolate launches — because a regular asked us to 'make it darker'
excerpt: One of our earliest regulars kept asking for a darker chocolate cookie. Priya Sharma, who joined as Flavor Architect in May, took the challenge personally. After 19 iterations blending three different cocoa percentages, the Double Dark Chocolate is here. Extra-bitter finish, and already our second bestseller.

title: Marcus Chen joins as Dough Master — the fermentation schedule just got serious
excerpt: Marcus comes to us from a 10-year career in artisan bread bakeries, where he managed sourdough schedules across three shifts. He's now applying that same rigor to our 48-hour cold fermentation. "Bread and cookies are cousins," he says. "Flour, water, time. The difference is butter, and a lot of it."

title: Introducing the Sea Salt Caramel — Lena's grandmother's recipe, 70 years in the making
excerpt: Our first new flavor since opening is a family heirloom. Lena adapted her grandmother's caramel recipe — originally from Brittany — into a brown-butter cookie base with fleur de sel de Guérande. "She'd be delighted and probably a little critical," Lena says. Available starting this weekend.

title: First month: 1,200 cookies, one broken mixer, and a waitlist that won't quit
excerpt: The numbers from our first 30 days are in. We shipped to 14 states, burned out one hand mixer (replaced with a proper Hobart), and hired our first employee — Sam O'Brien, who started as a dishwasher and within weeks was portioning dough alongside Lena. The waitlist now sits at 340. We're working through it as fast as quality allows.

title: Big Cookies opens its doors in southeast Portland
excerpt: Lena Kovač and Yuki Tanaka opened the bakery this morning at 6am in a 400-square-foot commercial kitchen with one oven and one mixer. By 10am, the first batch of Classic Chocolate Chunk sold out. By Friday, the waiting list hit 200. "We didn't expect this," Lena said. "We just wanted to make cookies the slow way."
